# Do11y

> Do11y is a documentation observability tool. It turns documentation usage into structured event data by streaming behavioral events (page views, scroll depth, link clicks, search queries, code-block copies, section reading time, tab switches, TOC clicks, feedback, and expand/collapse interactions) from your docs site to Supabase in real time.

Do11y is agent-native: it detects AI platform referrers (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, Copilot, DeepSeek, and others) so you can understand how agents and humans engage with your content differently.

The runtime artifact is a single dependency-free JavaScript file. It supports Mintlify, Docusaurus, Nextra, MkDocs Material, and VitePress out of the box. For other frameworks, manual setup is available via CDN or self-hosting.

Privacy: no cookies, no PII, no device fingerprinting, no cross-site tracking. Uses sessionStorage. No GDPR consent banner required.

## Docs

- [Introduction](https://docservable.com/introduction): What Do11y tracks, the privacy model, and supported frameworks.
- [Get started](https://docservable.com/get-started): Create a Supabase project and configure Do11y.
- [Manual setup](https://docservable.com/install/manual): CDN and self-hosting instructions for any framework.
- [Configuration](https://docservable.com/configuration): All configuration options including destination, behavioral options, framework values, and custom selectors.
- [Audit](https://docservable.com/audit): AI-powered recommendations about what to fix in your documentation.
- [SQL queries](https://docservable.com/queries): Ready-to-run SQL queries for traffic, engagement, navigation, content performance, and more.
- [Reference](https://docservable.com/reference): Events schema, AI traffic detection fields, JavaScript API, and known limitations.

## Optional

- [GitHub](https://github.com/manototh/do11y): Source code, releases, and issue tracker.
- [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/do11y): Package published as do11y.
- [Supabase](https://supabase.com): The platform that stores Do11y event data (PostgreSQL).
